description_long: This research institute is a modern, rectangular building elevated on slender columns, featuring a flat roof with a series of geometric forms and open terraces. The exterior is predominantly gray and white, with large horizontal windows running along the main facade, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The roof includes a mix of curved and angular structures, likely housing mechanical systems or specialized research spaces. A broad, green lawn and paved area extend from the main entrance, providing a welcoming approach and outdoor space. The building's design emphasizes clean lines, functional forms, and a minimalist aesthetic, typical of Modernist architecture. There is no visible signage or logo, and the entryway is subtly integrated into the facade.
description: A modern, rectangular research institute with a flat roof, large horizontal windows, and a minimalist gray-and-white exterior, elevated on columns and fronted by a green lawn.
description_view_0: Side view showing the building's elevation on columns, flat roof with geometric structures, and the adjacent green lawn.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance, broad paved approach, and the horizontal window band across the facade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the building's length, column supports, and the full extent of the green lawn.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the building's roof geometry and the open terrace areas, with less emphasis on the entrance.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is wide and flush with the facade, accessible via a broad paved path leading from the green lawn. It appears to have large glass doors integrated into the horizontal window band, with no visible steps, suggesting level access for accessibility. There are no decorative elements or prominent signage.
